skill.name: transparency-reporter
skill.description: Create transparent blocker, solution, health, and stakeholder reports for project teams.
skill.summary: Document blockers, fixes, and project health in clear traceable reports.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: Static command execution findings are false positives from fenced markdown report templates, not executable code. The sensitive, cryptography, reconnaissance, and hidden file findings are natural-language examples or paths inside sample reports. The only confirmed risk is low-impact guidance to write documentation files under project report directories.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Report Output Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to store blocker and transparency reports under project log and documentation directories. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it can create or update repository files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The report storage section clearly names project output directories. No evidence shows writes outside the workspace or destructive file operations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences
low_findings.description: The static Ruby shell backtick findings point to fenced markdown report templates and examples. They are not executable commands and do not include shell invocation instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are plain markdown fences around report text. No command interpreter, script body, or user-controlled execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Natural Language Security Terms
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, Windows SAM, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance alerts are triggered by ordinary documentation words. No evidence of credential access, cryptographic operations, or reconnaissance behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are headings or report template text. They do not reference SAM files, network scans, crypto algorithms, or system probing commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Illustrative Hidden Directory Path
low_findings.description: The hidden file access alert references a sample build failure path containing a .next directory. The line is part of an example report, not an instruction to read hidden files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path appears inside a good report example describing a build error. It does not instruct the assistant to access, modify, or exfiltrate hidden files.

content.value_statement: Project blockers often lose context as teams move between fixes and status updates. This skill creates clear blocker, solution, health, and stakeholder reports.
actual_capabilities: Creates structured blocker reports with impact, root cause, owner, and current state.
Documents resolved blockers with fixes, verification steps, risks, and lessons learned.
Generates periodic project health summaries with blocker, quality, and velocity metrics.
Formats stakeholder updates that separate completed, blocked, and in-progress work.
Defines severity levels for blocker triage and escalation.
Provides good and bad report examples for consistent team communication.
limitations: It does not collect metrics automatically from project systems.
It relies on the user or assistant to provide accurate blocker details.
It does not verify whether a reported fix actually works.
It may create documentation files when used with write access.

use_cases.title: Track Engineering Blockers
use_cases.description: Record what failed, who is affected, and what is needed before work can continue.

use_cases.title: Share Client Status
use_cases.description: Prepare direct updates that show completed work, active blockers, and next steps.

use_cases.title: Preserve Fix History
use_cases.description: Capture root causes, attempted solutions, verification steps, and prevention notes after each resolution.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Blocker Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner report for a newly found blocker.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a blocker report for this issue: [describe issue]. Include expected behavior, actual behavior, impact, severity, owner, and next step.

prompt_templates.title: Document a Fix
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate report after a blocker is resolved.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a solution report for blocker [id]. The fix was [summary]. Include files changed, why it works, verification, risks, and lessons learned.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Health Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a project health report for [date range]. Use these blocker, build, test, quality, and velocity details: [paste details].

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Stakeholder Transparency
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced update for clients or senior stakeholders.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a stakeholder status update from these project facts: [paste facts]. Separate completed work, blocked work, in-progress work, metrics, risks, and next week plan.

output_examples.input: A production build fails because a generated manifest directory is missing.
output_examples.output: A blocker report with failure details, deployment impact, attempted fixes, current owner, risk level, and a target resolution date.

output_examples.input: A build blocker was fixed by creating a missing directory before the build runs.
output_examples.output: A solution report explaining the fix, why it works, verification results, regression checks, and prevention notes.

output_examples.input: Weekly project facts include two active blockers, three completed fixes, and stable tests.
output_examples.output: A stakeholder update showing completed work, blocked items, active progress, health metrics, and the plan for the next week.

best_practices: Provide exact error messages, affected features, and current ownership before asking for a report.
Update reports when blocker state, severity, owner, or target date changes.
Keep facts separate from interpretation so stakeholders can trust the status.
anti_patterns: Do not use vague status labels without impact, owner, or next action.
Do not omit failed attempts, because they prevent repeated work.
Do not claim progress when the blocker still prevents delivery.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates blocker reports, solution reports, project health summaries, and stakeholder status updates.

faq.question: Does it run diagnostics automatically?
faq.answer: No. It organizes information provided by the user or another assistant into clear reports.

faq.question: Can it write files?
faq.answer: The skill describes report storage paths, so a tool with write access may create documentation files.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Engineering leads, project managers, and teams that need clear records of blockers and fixes.

faq.question: Does it replace incident management tools?
faq.answer: No. It creates structured reports that can be copied into existing tracking and incident tools.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the issue, expected result, actual result, impact, attempted fixes, owner, severity, and target resolution.
